problem_addressed: Evaluating machine learning agents on machine learning engineering tasks through a structured competition framework
core_claim: MLE-bench provides a standardized benchmark for assessing the capabilities of ML agents in performing ML engineering tasks
key_contributions: A comprehensive evaluation framework for ML agents on ML engineering tasks
A specific metric (Log of Mean Absolute Error) for evaluating predictions across multiple scalar coupling types
A structured competition setup with defined data, evaluation protocols, and timeline
novelty: The novelty lies in creating a standardized benchmark specifically for ML engineering tasks rather than traditional ML problems, with a focus on agent capabilities and reproducibility
method_summary: The paper describes a competition framework where ML agents predict scalar coupling constants between atom pairs in molecules, evaluated using a log-MAE metric averaged across coupling types
method_components: Data preprocessing from molecular structure files (XYZ and CSV formats)
Feature engineering from molecular properties (dipole moments, shielding tensors, charges, etc.)
Structured evaluation with log-MAE metric and specific submission format
key_innovation: The single most important innovation is the log-MAE evaluation metric that provides equal weight to improvements across different scalar coupling types, ensuring fair comparison
assumptions: Molecular structure data contains sufficient information for predicting scalar coupling constants
The provided supplementary data (dipole moments, shielding tensors, etc.) are relevant features
The log transformation of MAE appropriately handles the scale of coupling constants
theoretical_grounding: The paper establishes a theoretical floor of '1e-9' for MAE, resulting in a minimum possible score of approximately -20.7232 for perfect predictions
datasets_used: scalar coupling constant prediction dataset (train.csv, test.csv, structures.zip, structures.csv, dipole_moments.csv, magnetic_shielding_tensors.csv, mulliken_charges.csv, potential_energy.csv, scalar_coupling_contributions.csv)
headline_result: o1-preview achieved 34.3% medal rate on Low complexity competitions
best_result: o1-preview achieved 34.3% medal rate on Low complexity competitions
title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
